claim_boundary: Topology-hypothesis planning exposed alternate-route diagnostics and selected topology commands on the bottleneck slice, but did not improve the matched h160 progress outcome.
interpretation: Topology alternatives were available for 90 of 160 diagnostic steps and the topology command won 33 steps, so the mechanism is active enough to explain command-source changes on this bottleneck slice.
All topology-command wins selected the primary route and the explicit hypothesis switch count was 0, so this run does not show active switching between alternate route hypotheses.
The matched topology-guided step diagnostic had slightly worse final goal distance than the base row and no success within h160, so it does not mitigate the bottleneck failure under this proof step.
Both rows had zero collision flags and no fallback count; this is diagnostic adapter evidence, not benchmark-strength planner improvement.
